top_cluster.label: Pricing trust risk
top_cluster.why_it_matters: Pricing distrust converts quiet complaints into churn and lost sales.
top_cluster.opportunity: Publish proactive pricing guardrails and overage alerts before customers feel trapped.
representative_quotes: NimbusLedger looked cheap until the surprise overage bill.
Prospect likes the product, but they asked if pricing alerts exist before overages.

clusters.label: Pricing trust risk
clusters.why_it_matters: Pricing distrust converts quiet complaints into churn and lost sales.
clusters.opportunity: Publish proactive pricing guardrails and overage alerts before customers feel trapped.
representative_quotes: NimbusLedger looked cheap until the surprise overage bill.
Prospect likes the product, but they asked if pricing alerts exist before overages.

clusters.label: Integration reliability
clusters.why_it_matters: Integration bugs damage operational trust at the exact moment teams depend on the product.
clusters.opportunity: Ship idempotency controls, replay-safe webhooks, and customer-visible sync health.
representative_quotes: We only noticed after customers complained about missing invoice rows.

clusters.label: Onboarding confusion
clusters.why_it_matters: Confusing setup hides adoption friction until users abandon the product.
clusters.opportunity: Replace scattered setup instructions with a guided onboarding checklist and failed-import evidence.
representative_quotes: The dashboard is useful after setup, but onboarding is confusing.
The import wizard says success even when three records fail validation.

clusters.label: Executive visibility gap
clusters.why_it_matters: Teams lose the pattern when complaints are split across communities, support, and sales notes.
clusters.opportunity: Create a weekly review digest that translates scattered complaints into product decisions.
representative_quotes: Pricing is technically documented but hidden behind three clicks.
We need a weekly executive digest of complaints.

clusters.label: Competitor pressure
clusters.why_it_matters: Competitor mentions are buying signals disguised as casual feedback.
clusters.opportunity: Turn competitor wins into a counter-positioning roadmap with proof-backed gaps.
representative_quotes: Prospect likes the product, but they asked if pricing alerts exist before overages.

clusters.label: Customer love
clusters.why_it_matters: Positive feedback is useful, but it should not bury emerging product risk.
clusters.opportunity: Preserve what customers already value while watching for new hidden complaints.
representative_quotes: The dashboard is useful after setup, but onboarding is confusing.
Competitor LedgerFox won their previous deal because it had spend guardrails and clearer review evidence.

action_plan.title: Launch pricing-alert guardrails and rewrite hidden overage language
action_plan.expected_impact: Reduce churn risk and rescue expansion conversations before finance escalates.

action_plan.title: Add replay-safe webhook diagnostics and duplicate invoice protection
action_plan.expected_impact: Protect operational trust for finance teams during month-end workflows.

action_plan.title: Ship guided onboarding with visible failed-import evidence
action_plan.expected_impact: Shorten time to value and stop silent setup failures from becoming public complaints.

action_plan.title: Send weekly hidden-review digest to product and founder stakeholders
action_plan.expected_impact: Expose cross-channel patterns before they become churn.

action_plan.title: Create competitor-loss review board and counter-positioning copy
action_plan.expected_impact: Turn lost-deal language into roadmap and sales enablement.

action_plan.title: Instrument public shadow-channel monitoring
action_plan.expected_impact: Keep Reddit, GitHub, community, and app-store pain visible in one review cockpit.

action_plan.title: Create a customer-proof changelog tied to review themes
action_plan.expected_impact: Show customers that hidden feedback becomes shipped improvements.

recommended_tier.name: Growth
recommended_tier.target_customer: SaaS teams with public communities and support queues

founder_summary.one_line: hidden.reviews finds the reviews customers never put in one place.
founder_summary.domain_connection: The domain is literal: hidden.reviews becomes the command center for buried customer truth.
founder_summary.buyer: Founders, PMs, and customer success teams at SaaS companies with fragmented feedback channels.
founder_summary.why_now: AI lets teams scrape, cluster, and operationalize feedback faster than manual review meetings.
